items.title: New initiative urges personal pledges to overcome 'overwhelming' DV stats

children.content: A new platform, dubbed Justice For Her, is centred on making small, actionable pledges to help people overcome the overwhelming statistics on gendered violence, which often lead to inaction.

gallery.altText: Sally Haynes and Carolyn Self launching "Justice for Her" 2026-04-08 10:04:00

thumbnail.altText: Sally Haynes and Carolyn Self launching "Justice for Her" 2026-04-08 10:04:00

items.title: 'Made it very attractive': Free rides luring thousands more onto buses

children.content: The temporary ability to catch public transport for free is turning some Tasmanians into "converts", while others are using it for new experiences.

image.altText: A man in a 'PROTECT TAKAYNA' shirt stands in front of a bus going to Devonport.

items.title: Three-day hearing set for Grace Tame abuser over alleged 'menacing' social posts

children.content: Nicolaas Bester, who was previously found guilty of sexually abusing Grace Tame, has been deemed unfit to stand trial over allegations he made "menacing" posts directed at her on social media.

items.title: Singapore's PM knows Australia is vulnerable but Albanese has leverage

children.content: Anthony Albanese is flying to Singapore as part of a hastily arranged regional mission to shore up crucial fuel supplies, amid warnings the global energy crisis could "sharply turn for the worse".

image.altText: Two men shake hands in front of two flags

items.title: Uni in financial crisis spent nearly $1.5m on leadership coach, documents show

children.content: Documents released to Four Corners show the University of Technology Sydney spent hundreds of thousands of dollars a year on a leadership coach before planning to slash jobs and courses to save money.

gallery.altText: Two modern university buildings seen across a road with several cars. People can be seen in one of the glass buildings.

thumbnail.altText: Two modern university buildings seen across a road with several cars. People can be seen in one of the glass buildings.

items.title: US architect admits killing eight women in Gilgo Beach murders

children.content: Rex Heuermann pleads guilty to seven murders and admits to the killing of another woman, solving a notorious case known as the Gilgo Beach killings.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit and tie smiling in a court room.

items.title: 'Ketamine Queen' jailed for 15 years over death of Friends star Matthew Perry

children.content: Jayvee Sangha admits to running a "stash house" that helped supply the famous Friends star ketamine in the months before his death.

image.altText: A picture of Matthew Perry at a film premiere.

items.title: Family still waits to know how their loved one died, one year on

children.content: The family of Indigenous man Kumanjayi White has criticised the secrecy around the independent review into his death, which has been handed to NT Police and the DPP but not released publicly.

image.altText: An illustration of an Indigenous man with dark hair and a beard, standing on a red dirt landscape and wearing a yellow t-shirt.

items.title: Brisbane suburbs set to become 'mini CBDs' as city prepares for population surge

children.content: As Brisbane prepares for an expected surge in population over the next 15 years, the council has introduced a plan to allow high-rise apartments in some of the city's suburban hubs.

image.altText: A sand coloured block of apartments

items.title: Mining on the Moon is closer than ever, but challenges remain

children.content: A scientist, an engineer, an archaeologist and a lawyer break down some of the physical, environmental, ethical and legal challenges of mining on the Moon.

gallery.altText: The surface of the Moon, with Earth rising in the background.
A moon rover with a night sky background.
A robot with caterpillar wheels and arms on a mound of dust in front of an American flag.

thumbnail.altText: The surface of the Moon, with Earth rising in the background.
A moon rover with a night sky background.
A robot with caterpillar wheels and arms on a mound of dust in front of an American flag.

items.title: Egg-laying hens in cages set for better living conditions under new laws

children.content: The lives of caged hens are set to improve as Victoria implements new welfare laws, but farmers say they have not been consulted.

image.altText: A hen inside a cage inside a poultry farm

items.title: Distance triples and comeback kings end Aus Open swimming

children.content: Sam Williamson capped his comeback with victory in the 50m breaststroke just 11 months after he suffered a career-threatening injury.

items.title: A 'bunch of nobodies' changed Australia — now a new generation carries their torch

children.content: When a "bunch of nobodies" in Sydney decided to stand up to police brutality, no-one could guess their impact would still be felt more than 50 years later.

gallery.altText: A black and white photo of people standing in front of a shopfront.
People with pickets that read 'Moree NSW Land Rights Now' stand.

thumbnail.altText: A black and white photo of people standing in front of a shopfront.
People with pickets that read 'Moree NSW Land Rights Now' stand.

items.title: Failed referendum inspires two top dance companies to join forces

children.content: Sixteen dancers from Bangarra Dance Theatre and 19 ballet dancers from the Australian Ballet have joined forces for Flora, choreographed by Bangarra artistic director Frances Rings.

image.altText: On stage, an Indigenous dancer leans back, stretching one arm up. Behind her other dancers in shadow form a line.

items.title: Test your knowledge with our classic Australian TV quiz

children.content: One quote, two possible answers: test your knowledge and take a nostalgic trip down memory lane as you match the quote to the iconic Australian television series.

image.altText: Ray Meagher on the set of Home and Away playing Alf Stewart.

items.title: How a dog fence in Coburg ended in conflict resolution workshops

children.content: A controversial fence designed to keep dogs out of a park in Melbourne's north has been removed after community opposition.

gallery.altText: The words stop the fence spray painted on a tree.

thumbnail.altText: The words stop the fence spray painted on a tree.

items.title: 'The whole industry will fall over': Transport leaders call for urgent action

children.content: An emergency Fair Work Commission hearing in Sydney begins, with unions and employers in the transport industry saying drivers, employees and businesses are on the verge of going under, calling on the top of the supply chain to share the load.

image.altText: A large silver truck drives off into a pink sunset.

items.title: What is financial stress and how does it affect our decisions?

children.content: If financial stress is causing distractions — for example, you're worrying about money when you should be concentrating on driving or work — there might be something deeper happening.

image.altText: Lady holding a wallet with 20 dollar bills.

items.title: Human bones found on Rottnest Island, investigation underway

children.content: Police are investigating the origin of what are believed to be several human bones that were found on Rottnest Island.

image.altText: A police vehicle parked on a building site.

items.title: First person charged with 'from the river to the sea' offence faces court

children.content: Liam Parry, 33, was arrested and charged last month for reciting the banned phrase "from the river to the sea."

image.altText: A man speaks in front of several microphones

items.title: Police failures exposed during inquest into 50 year cold case

children.content: Ms Green was last seen alive on February 13, 1976, with the Coroner's Court of Victoria hearing the litany of policing errors following her disappearance.

gallery.altText: A black and white photo of Veronica Green who wears a long white dress.

thumbnail.altText: A black and white photo of Veronica Green who wears a long white dress.

avatar.alt: A man with dark skin in a suit looks at the camera

items.title: Former coach says AFL 'shamed' him over $20K fine

children.content: Ex-Port Adelaide coach Ken Hinkley, who was fined for sledging in 2024, says he felt exploited by the league's later use of footage of his theatrics as a promotional tool.

image.altText: Coach celebrates with players, staff, in front of fans, at full-time of an AFL match

items.title: The three reasons behind Penrith's unstoppable start to 2026

children.content: Already, the 2026 premiership looks like it's Penrith's to lose, and there are three big reasons behind the Panthers' record-breaking start to the season.

image.altText: Tom Jenkins celebrates a try in a rugby league match

items.title: Amid 'unprecedented' times, farmers take 'risky' gamble on early winter crops

children.content: Some SA farmers have rolled the dice on planting winter crops weeks earlier than usual thanks to record-breaking rains. Now they hope the gamble pays off.

image.altText: A drone shot of dry seeding in a paddock near Jamestown, in SA's mid north in May 2025

items.title: Farmers angered by plans to turn coal mine into lake bigger than Sydney Harbour

children.content: There are plans to turn the former Hazelwood coal mine in Victoria into a lake bigger than Sydney Harbour, but farmers and environmentalists are worried about the impact on nearby waterways.

image.altText: A young man with a beard, wearing a blue shirt and cap stands alongside a riverbed.

items.title: 'Offensive', 'degrading': Court documents reveal Jackie O's complaints

children.content: Court documents allege Jackie "O" Henderson was subject to "offensive and degrading" comments about her personal and dating life from co-host Kyle Sandilands in two separate incidents last year.

gallery.altText: A selfie of Jackie O Henderson smiling with Kyle Sandilands in the background wearing radio headphones.

thumbnail.altText: A selfie of Jackie O Henderson smiling with Kyle Sandilands in the background wearing radio headphones.
